articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Major Anxiety Among Landlords:** 82% of landlords are worried about the implications of the Renters’ Rights Act, with 44% expressing significant concern.  
- **Shift in Investment Strategies:** 75% say increasing regulation has deterred portfolio growth, and nearly half (49%) plan to sell or downsize their holdings soon.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are urged to enhance energy performance certificate (EPC) ratings to attract tenants, with a new mandate to achieve a minimum C rating by 2030 on the horizon.  
- Increasing energy costs, fueled by geopolitical tensions, have heightened tenants' demand for energy-efficient properties to cut their living expenses.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Less than 25% of UK landlords believe planning reforms will benefit their businesses, highlighting widespread skepticism.  
- Concerns about the effectiveness of proposed reforms indicate a lack of confidence in improving the rental market.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks in properties come from external sources, emphasizing the need for landlords to prioritize outside pipework maintenance.  
- Neglecting these external leaks could lead to costly repairs, as highlighted by leak detection experts at Aspect.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters’ Rights Act, effective May 1, 2026, will revolutionize the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales, enhancing tenant protections while mandating new responsibilities for landlords.  
- Landlords should prepare now by following expert tips to navigate the impending regulatory changes successfully.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 84% of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act set to take effect on May 1.  
- Nearly 58% of landlords face a high risk of non-compliance with the new legislation.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are shifting strategies to restructure portfolios instead of pursuing expansion as rental growth slows, reflecting changing market demands.
- The ONS highlights a slowdown in rental growth, prompting landlords to prioritize portfolio optimization and financing adjustments.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey reveals landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.
- Concerns grow as rising competition from landlords limits opportunities for new homeowners.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the next government to acknowledge the significance of the private rented sector (PRS) with a formal, dedicated strategy.  
- There is a call to action to prevent the decline of landlords in Scotland, emphasizing the need for government support.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting in May, landlords must be aware of new HMRC regulations requiring tax advisors to be registered, or risk facing penalties of up to £10,000.  
- Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are particularly at risk of non-compliance under these mandatory registration rules.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council asserts that implementing licensing for landlords will not result in increased rental prices.
- Landlords face a £694 fee per property, which the council does not plan to reimburse.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour leader Matthew Pennycook has explicitly stated that the party will not implement rent controls.  
- The announcement signals a shift away from tenants' demands for greater housing affordability measures.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council aims to implement stricter regulations to curb the unregulated growth of Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs).  
- Detailed guidelines for HMO landlords are in the works to promote better management and standards.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign kicks off on Monday to educate tenants on leveraging the Renters Rights Act.  
- The initiative aims to empower renters by providing knowledge and resources to maximize their rights.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- Experts warn that the new Renters Rights Act is leading to a significant increase in S21 eviction notices.
- Paul Shamplina had previously forecasted this trend, highlighting concerns for tenant stability.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are increasing as the Renters Rights Act approaches, indicating a potential market shift.  
- New data from Hamptons reveals this trend, highlighting the urgency for renters amid changing legislation.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The impending Renters Rights Act, set to be enacted soon, is expected to significantly reduce the viability for hobbyist landlords in the UK property market.
- This legislation is anticipated to reshape the rental landscape, leading to fewer small-scale landlords and potentially tightening housing supply.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly venturing into property investment, navigating challenges posed by regulatory burdens and taxation.  
- This trend highlights a shift in investment strategies among younger generations despite a challenging market landscape.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Sellers are facing a challenging market, with many having to reduce asking prices, experience failed sales, or withdraw their properties altogether.  
- House Buyer Bureau's Chris Hodgkinson highlights the increasing difficulties for sellers navigating the current real estate landscape.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is increasing, boosting Inheritance Tax receipts.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations for Inheritance Tax appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC intensifies oversight on property valuations, leading to a rise in cases sent to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).  
- Enhanced scrutiny is contributing to a boost in Inheritance Tax revenues.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.

articles.ai_summary:

- The unusual oversight has sparked attention, highlighting the quirks of real estate marketing practices.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.

articles.ai_summary:

- The fees are described as an "unseen surcharge," adding financial strain to the home-buying process.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.

articles.ai_summary:

- David Alexander from Lomond highlights the strategic move to bolster their presence north of the border.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.
The post Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35% appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:

- CEO Guy Gittins highlights the ongoing struggles in the market, emphasizing a significant slowdown in new buyer engagement.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices

Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.

articles.ai_summary:
- Inflation surges as escalating Middle East conflict pushes up fuel and transport costs.  
- Experts warn that rising prices are likely to affect other sectors of the economy soon.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Limited Market Exposure:** Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto, reveals that 90% of new homes are never made available on the open sales market.  
- **Market Dynamics Shift:** This trend highlights a significant disconnect in the housing market, affecting availability and pricing for potential buyers.
